title: ISKCON Temple Patna
tagline: ISKCON Temple Patna — spiritual town of Bihar

overview.short: The International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON), commonly known as the Hare Krishna Movement, is a Hindu religious organization.
overview.description: The International Society for Krishna Consciousness (ISKCON), commonly known as the Hare Krishna Movement, is a Hindu religious organization. It follows the Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition, which emphasizes devotion (bhakti) to Krishna as the supreme deity. The ISKCON was founded on 13 July 1966 in New York City by A. C. Bhaktivedanta Swami Prabhupada. The organization's spiritual and administrative headquarters is located in Mayapur, West Bengal, India, and it claims a global membership of around one million people.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Buddha Smriti Park
topPlaces.description: Buddha Smriti Park is also known as Buddha Memorial Park (as translated in English) is an urban park located on Frazer Road near Patna Junction in Patna, India. This park has been designed by Vikram Lall and developed by the Bihar Government to commemorate the 2554th birth anniversary of the Buddha.

topPlaces.name: Bailey Road, Patna
topPlaces.description: Bailey Road (also known as Nehru Path) is a road and neighbourhood in Patna, India. It connects Patna with Danapur.

topPlaces.name: Dak Bungalow Crossing, Patna
topPlaces.description: Dak Bungalow Crossing is a major commercial intersection, entertainment center and neighborhood in the central section of Patna. It is located at the junction of Bailey Road and Fraser Road.

topPlaces.name: Mahavir Mandir
topPlaces.description: Mahavir Mandir is a Hindu temple dedicated to the god Hanuman, located in Patna, Bihar, India. Millions of pilgrims visit the temple every year.

topPlaces.name: Kidwaipuri
topPlaces.description: Kidwaipuri, also known as P&T Colony, is a neighbourhood of Patna. It is centrally located residential colony of Patna, adjacent to Nageshwar Colony.

topPlaces.name: General Post Office, Patna
topPlaces.description: The General Post Office, Patna (abbreviation GPO, commonly known as the Patna GPO) is a British Raj building located in Patna, Bihar, India. It is the central post office of the city of Patna and the headquarters of India Post's Bihar Circle.

topPlaces.name: Beer Chand Patel Path
topPlaces.description: Beer Chand Patel Path (formerly Gardiner Road) is major thoroughfare in Patna. It branches off from Income Tax Golamber and terminates at R-Block Golamber.

topPlaces.name: Bankipore
topPlaces.description: Bankipur (Hindi: बाँकीपुर) is a neighbourhood and residential area in Patna, in the Indian state of Bihar. It is located on the bank of the river Ganges.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit ISKCON Temple Patna?
faq.a: The best time to visit ISKCON Temple Patna is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach ISKCON Temple Patna?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jay Prakash Narayan Airport, Patna (5 km) and the nearest railway station is Patna Junction (1 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for ISKCON Temple Patna?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of ISKCON Temple Patna at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in ISKCON Temple Patna?
faq.a: Stays in ISKCON Temple Patna start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in ISKCON Temple Patna?
faq.a: The top attractions are Buddha Smriti Park, Bailey Road, Patna, Dak Bungalow Crossing, Patna, Mahavir Mandir, Kidwaipuri.
